negative_memory.hypothesis_tested: fold-local intraday range acceptance failure reversal rank over boundary probe depth, acceptance failure recoil, failed followthrough distance, opposing tail rejection, boundary reclaim strength, volume exhaustion divergence, session range extension stress, opening range reclaim context, prior-day boundary failure context, time reversal alignment, session phase maturity, and spread stress inverse features
negative_memory.next_boundary: C0106 R0001 shows the tested range acceptance failure reversal surface is not a candidate as implemented; remaining C0106 variants would be adjacent tuning, so next work should choose a new major C0107 hypothesis without selected, economics-pass, runtime, materialization, ONNX, promotion, or live claims
negative_memory.reason_not_candidate: activity landed inside the target band, but proxy, closed-bar MT5 logic, aggregate MT5 tick, and fold-isolated MT5 tick evidence did not establish a candidate

supporting_lessons.summary: R0001 produced 5 profitable and 4 losing fold-isolated MT5 tick folds, total tick net PnL -42.24, and worst fold rw_007 net PnL -30.09.
Schedule replay mechanical parity passed with mismatch_count 0, so the weak result is not explained by proxy-vs-MT5 logic mismatch.
Tick execution improved closed-bar logic by 43.32 total net PnL across 8 better and 1 worse folds, but both logic and tick surfaces remained negative.
Native MQL signal logic, ONNX materialization, cost/slippage hardening, final sizing, and historical lineage debt remain unresolved; no stronger claim is created.

required_kpi_summary.remaining_work_classification: no_candidate; further C0106 work would be adjacent threshold, stop, target, hold, session, activity, spread, capital, or range-acceptance-failure-reversal feature nudge tuning

hypothesis_variant.summary: fold-local intraday range acceptance failure reversal rank run
hypothesis_variant.variant_boundary: C0106 R0001 tests whether a probe beyond an intraday range or session boundary that fails to gain acceptance, reclaims the boundary, shows opposing-tail rejection, and loses volume participation can form a target-first reversal surface after realistic spread cost. It changes feature family, label conditioning, model context, and trade-logic state key from C0105 close pressure followthrough. It is not a threshold/window/SL/TP/session/spread/capital nudge, materialization step, ONNX step, selected claim, runtime claim, or retry-only run.

pre_open_decision.adjacent_tuning_rejection_reason: No C0105 thresholds, windows, stops, targets, sessions, spread gates, feature nudges, or capital sizing changes are used as the run question.

surface_distance_rationale.feature: Boundary probe depth, failure recoil, reclaim strength, volume exhaustion, opening range context, and prior-day boundary context replace C0105 close-pressure-followthrough features.
surface_distance_rationale.label: Target-first quality after range acceptance failure reversal, not target-first quality after close pressure followthrough.
surface_distance_rationale.model: Fold-local linear range acceptance failure reversal rank.
surface_distance_rationale.trade_logic: Top fold-local range-acceptance-failure-reversal state scores per active day.

pre_open_decision.true_variant_summary: New major campaign surface asks whether failed range acceptance after a boundary probe carries target-first reversal edge after realistic spread cost.

feature.summary: boundary probe depth; acceptance failure recoil; failed followthrough distance; opposing tail rejection; boundary reclaim strength; volume exhaustion divergence; session range extension stress; opening range reclaim context; prior-day boundary failure context; time reversal alignment; session phase maturity; spread stress inverse.

label.summary: Target-first quality after range acceptance failure reversal.

model.summary: Fold-local linear intraday range acceptance failure reversal rank.

trade_logic.summary: Dual-direction top fold-local range-acceptance-failure-reversal scores per active day using next-bar-open fixed-lot discovery entries.
